description: The benchmark all-mountain board. Directional twin shape with camber delivers powerful edge hold and versatile performance across the whole mountain.
full_description: The Burton Custom is the board that has defined all-mountain riding for decades. Its directional twin shape provides symmetry needed for switch riding while the slight taper toward the tail gives a more intuitive feel going straight. Full camber underfoot delivers serious power and edge hold that rewards committed, energetic riding. The sintered base is fast and durable, while the Off-Axis laminates create distinct torsion characteristics that feel uniquely responsive. Whether you're carving hardpack groomers, navigating trees, hitting natural features, or riding the whole mountain top to bottom, the Custom handles it all with confidence.
why_recommended: Your riding style, ability level, and weight all align perfectly with what the Custom is designed for. Its medium-stiff flex suits riders who want control without sacrificing energy, and the camber profile matches your preference for an assertive, connected feel. The directional twin shape gives you versatility across the terrain you selected.

description: A forgiving all-mountain freestyle board with a twin shape and Flying V hybrid profile. Great for riders developing their skills across the whole mountain.
full_description: The Burton Process is one of the most versatile and approachable all-mountain boards Burton makes. The true twin shape means it rides identically in both directions — great for riders learning switch or who enjoy a symmetrical feel. The Flying V hybrid profile combines rocker between the feet for a playful, buttery feel with camber under each foot for edge hold and pop. It's a board that wants to play but won't punish you when things get scrappy. The medium flex hits a sweet spot — soft enough to press and butter, stiff enough to hold an edge on steeper terrain. This is a board you can genuinely grow with.
why_recommended: Your ability level and preference for a playful, forgiving ride match the Process well. The hybrid profile suits riders who want to explore freestyle without committing to a full park board, and the medium flex gives you room to progress without feeling outgrown too quickly.

description: A directional freeride board built for charging hard on any terrain. Stiff camber profile rewards committed riding with powerful edge-to-edge response.
full_description: The Burton Deep Thinker is a no-nonsense freeride machine built for riders who want to charge. The directional shape and 10mm setback naturally keep weight over the tail, helping the nose float in variable snow and powder while keeping the tail locked in on hardpack. Full camber means zero wasted energy — every ounce of input translates directly into edge pressure and speed. The stiff flex rewards commitment and rewards hard turns at speed. It's not a board for the timid, but for intermediate-advanced riders with freeride ambitions, it's one of the most satisfying boards Burton makes.
why_recommended: You selected freeride as your riding style and have the ability level to get the most from this board's demanding but rewarding character. The stiff camber flex matches your preference for an aggressive, connected feel, and your weight sits comfortably in the ideal range.

description: A dedicated powder board with a massive rockered nose and setback stance. Surfy and effortless in deep snow with a tapered tail for easy pivoting.
full_description: The Burton Family Tree Straight Chuter is purpose-built for one thing: deep snow. The enormous rockered nose lifts out of powder almost effortlessly, while the 25mm setback stance keeps your weight back naturally so the nose stays up without effort. The tapered tail is shorter and narrower than the nose, creating easy pivoting and a genuinely surfy feeling when you're waist-deep in fresh. Medium flex keeps things fun and responsive. This is a pow-day weapon that will make you grin from first chair to last when conditions are deep.
why_recommended: Powder riding is at the top of your terrain priorities, and this board is designed from the ground up to deliver the ultimate deep snow experience. Your ability level means you'll be able to exploit what it does best, and the surfy feel you selected matches this board's core character perfectly.

description: Jones's all-mountain workhorse. Directional twin camber with Traction Tech 2.0 sintered base delivers grip and speed on any groomed or off-piste terrain.
full_description: The Jones Mountain Twin is the board that put Jones on the map and has kept them there. Built around a directional twin shape with a subtle 5mm setback, it strikes an ideal balance between switch-riding versatility and directional power. Full camber provides explosive pop and precise edge hold, while the Traction Tech 2.0 sintered base provides exceptional grip on hard snow and ice without sacrificing glide on softer terrain. The medium-stiff flex is demanding enough to reward advanced inputs but accessible enough for intermediate riders pushing their boundaries. The quality of construction is genuinely excellent.
why_recommended: Jones Mountain Twin aligns with your all-mountain style and ability level. The Traction Tech base is particularly well-suited if your terrain choices include any icy or technical conditions. The medium-stiff camber flex matches your preference for a connected, balanced feel.

description: A surfy powder board with a wide rockered nose and tapered tail. Incredibly playful in deep snow and lighter than most dedicated powder shapes.
full_description: The Jones Hovercraft earns its name. The wide, heavily rockered nose genuinely hovers above deep snow with minimal effort, while the pronounced taper toward the tail keeps pivoting and direction changes effortless. At medium flex, it's softer and more playful than many powder boards, making it approachable for intermediate-advanced riders building their deep-snow skills. It's also one of the lightest boards in its category. When conditions are deep and you want to feel like you're surfing the mountain, the Hovercraft delivers that feeling better than almost anything else at its price point.
why_recommended: Your desire for a surfy, playful powder ride and the terrain preferences you selected point directly at the Hovercraft. It's more accessible than pure expert boards while still delivering a genuine deep-snow experience, ideal for your ability level and weight range.

description: An ultra-light, ultra-stiff freeride twin for advanced riders. Carbon construction and full camber deliver explosive pop and precise edge hold at speed.
full_description: The Jones Ultra Mountain Twin is for advanced riders who want to charge hard and go fast without compromises. Carbon fibre laminates make it significantly lighter than comparable boards, reducing swing weight and making it more responsive to subtle inputs at high speed. The full camber profile is aggressive — this board wants to be ridden hard. The stiff flex and strong Traction Tech edges keep you planted and precise even on steep, technical terrain. The directional twin shape means switch riding is still possible, but make no mistake: this is a board designed primarily for pointing it downhill and committing.
why_recommended: Your advanced ability, preference for an aggressive ride, and freeride focus make the Ultra Mountain Twin a natural choice. The carbon construction and high flex rating suit riders who have the skills to push it and want a board that responds instantly to every input at speed.

description: Travis Rice's pro model. One of the stiffest, most technical freeride boards available with Magne-Traction serrated edges for grip in any condition.
full_description: The Lib Tech Travis Rice Pro is exactly what it sounds like — the board used by one of the most progressive big-mountain riders in the world. Magne-Traction serrated edges provide grip that feels almost supernatural on hardpack and ice, while the full camber and ultra-stiff flex demand absolute commitment in return. The 15mm setback keeps the nose high. This is a board that will outlast you technically — there's no ceiling here for most riders. It's not playful, it's not forgiving, but in the right conditions and in skilled hands, it's one of the most exhilarating boards you can ride.
why_recommended: Your advanced ability, aggressive preferred feel, and freeride focus put you in the target audience for this board. The Magne-Traction edge technology is a genuine advantage if your terrain choices include technical or icy conditions, and your weight falls comfortably in its optimal range.

description: The board that defined the rocker revolution. Buttery soft and effortlessly playful, the Skate Banana is brilliant for beginners and freestyle junkies alike.
full_description: The Lib Tech Skate Banana practically invented modern rocker snowboarding and remains one of the most fun boards ever made. Full continuous rocker means the edges barely touch the snow — you can pivot, butter, and slash without catching and without fear. The soft flex makes pressing and jibbing feel effortless and learning new tricks approachable. Magne-Traction edges provide more grip than the rocker profile might suggest. This is the board skateboarders fall in love with, brilliant for beginners who want to explore what's possible without intimidation.
why_recommended: For your ability level and preference for a playful, freestyle-oriented feel, the Skate Banana is hard to beat. The full rocker makes learning forgiving and fun, and the soft flex encourages creativity. It's a board that will put a smile on your face every single run.

description: A versatile powder-oriented board with a huge nose and early taper. Floats effortlessly in deep snow while remaining surprisingly capable on groomers.
full_description: The Lib Tech Cold Brew occupies a clever middle ground between dedicated powder boards and all-mountain riders. The huge rockered nose floats in deep snow with minimal effort, while the hybrid profile — rocker in the nose, camber underfoot — gives it edge hold and grip that pure powder boards can't match when you venture onto groomed terrain. Magne-Traction serrated edges add grip you wouldn't expect from a powder-oriented board. At medium flex, it's playful and forgiving. It's the board for riders who want to be ready for a powder day without struggling on days when conditions aren't perfect.
why_recommended: Your powder preference combined with the need for versatility makes the Cold Brew a smart choice. The hybrid profile ensures it won't punish you on hardpack while still delivering the floaty, surfy experience in deep snow that your terrain selection suggests you're after.

description: A beloved all-mountain freestyle twin with a versatile hybrid profile. Energetic and fun from park to powder, one of the most popular boards year after year.
full_description: The Capita Defenders of Awesome has been one of the most consistently popular all-mountain freestyle boards for years. The true twin shape rides identically in both directions, making it a dream for riders who enjoy switch. The Mercy hybrid profile combines rocker in the nose and tail for playfulness with camber underfoot for pop and edge hold. Medium flex gives it enough backbone for carving groomers without being so stiff it punishes park and freestyle riding. Built sustainably in Austria at Capita's Mothership factory, it also has genuine environmental credentials.
why_recommended: The DOA matches your intermediate ability and balanced, playful ride preference across all terrain. Its versatile hybrid profile means it adapts to whatever the mountain throws at you without demanding perfection from the rider.

description: A high-performance freeride twin for advanced riders. Full camber and ultralight construction deliver explosive response and precision at high speed.
full_description: The Capita DOA Ultrafear takes everything that makes the standard DOA great and strips it down to pure performance. Ultralight construction using carbon fibre laminates reduces swing weight without sacrificing structural integrity, creating a board that responds almost telepathically to subtle body language at speed. Full camber is unapologetically aggressive. Stiff flex and strong edge hold keep it planted and precise on the steepest terrain. The directional twin shape allows switch riding for riders who want that versatility. This is the board for advanced riders with freeride ambitions and no time for compromises.
why_recommended: Your advanced ability, aggressive ride preference, and freeride focus put the Ultrafear squarely in its target demographic. The ultralight carbon construction is a genuine advantage for riders charging hard and looking for maximum responsiveness at high speed.

description: A dedicated park and pipe board with a poppy camber underfoot and soft-to-medium flex. Symmetrical twin for switch riding and consistent pop off any feature.
full_description: The Capita Super DOA is a purpose-built park board that doesn't apologise for what it is. The true twin shape is completely symmetrical, so every hit and every landing feels exactly the same whether you're going forwards or switch. The camber underfoot is poppy and energetic — load it up on a jump and it'll launch you with authority. Soft-to-medium flex keeps jibs and rails playful without sacrificing too much energy when you need pop for bigger airs. It rewards technical riders who want to dial in tricks and benefit from consistent, predictable behaviour off every feature.
why_recommended: Park is your primary focus and you have the ability level to work with the Super DOA's energetic camber profile. The symmetrical twin shape and poppy flex suit your desire for a playful ride that rewards technical progression.

description: A carbon-laced all-mountain freestyle twin with GNU's Magnetraction edges. Lively and responsive with excellent edge grip for its playful character.
full_description: The GNU Carbon Credit is a medium-flex all-mountain freestyle twin that punches above its weight thanks to carbon lace laminates. These add snap and responsiveness without dramatically increasing stiffness, creating a board that feels energetic and alive underfoot. GNU's Magnetraction sinusoidal edges deliver edge hold that seems disproportionate to the board's playful character, keeping you connected on hardpack while the hybrid profile keeps things fun. It's excellent for intermediate-advanced riders who want to ride everything without feeling compromised anywhere.
why_recommended: The Carbon Credit matches your all-mountain riding style and ability level well. The Magnetraction edges address edge hold concerns that arise with more playful boards, making it a genuinely versatile choice for the terrain mix you selected.

description: A super soft, buttery park board ideal for jibbing, rails, and learning tricks. Rocker profile makes pressing and pivoting effortless.
full_description: The GNU Money is exactly what it promises: a soft, fun, jib-focused park board that makes learning tricks feel achievable and natural. Full rocker means the edges barely engage the snow, making pressing, pivoting and nose/tail butters feel effortless. The super soft flex bends easily, letting you get into presses without fighting the board. It's not built for big jumps or charging at speed, but on rails, boxes, and flat ground features, it's one of the most enjoyable boards you can ride. Great for intermediate riders who primarily want to progress their jib game.
why_recommended: Your intermediate ability and park-focused terrain selection make the Money a natural recommendation. The soft flex and full rocker are purpose-built for the jib and rail riding that suits your current ability level and preferred style.

description: Never Summer's flagship freeride board with their unique Rocker Camber Rocker profile. Exceptionally versatile — charges hard on groomers and floats in powder.
full_description: The Never Summer Proto Type Two is one of the most genuinely versatile boards on the market. The unique Rocker Camber Rocker profile — with rocker in the nose and tail, and camber between the bindings — delivers the best of both worlds: the nose lifts naturally in powder while the camber section gives edge hold and pop on hardpack. Stiff flex and strong edges make it an excellent charger on technical terrain. Built in Denver, Colorado using high-quality materials, Never Summer boards are famous for their longevity and precision. This is a board that will grow with you for years.
why_recommended: The Proto Type Two's unique hybrid profile matches your need for a board that works across multiple terrain types. Your ability and preference for an aggressive, connected feel are well-served by its stiff flex and strong edges, while the powder-friendly design handles deep days.

description: A dedicated carving and piste board with a stiff full-camber profile. Narrow waist and powerful edge hold designed for high-speed precision on hardpack.
full_description: The Never Summer Swift is a purpose-designed carving and piste board for riders who want to lay down clean arcs at high speed. The narrow waist gives immediate edge-to-edge transitions, and the full camber profile means every bit of energy you put in gets transferred directly to the edge. Stiff flex rewards technique — the better your carving fundamentals, the more this board rewards you. If groomed runs and hardpack are your playground and you want to feel every sensation of a clean carved turn, this board is built for you.
why_recommended: Piste riding is your focus and the Swift is designed exclusively for this purpose. Your ability level and preference for an aggressive ride are perfectly matched to its demanding but deeply rewarding character on hard snow.

description: Rome's do-everything freestyle board. A soft-to-medium twin with a playful hybrid profile that excels in the park and makes everyday riding more fun.
full_description: The Rome Artifact is Rome's go-to all-mountain freestyle board, and it earns that title by being genuinely fun without making demands of the rider. The true twin shape and soft-to-medium flex create a canvas that encourages creativity — pressing, buttering, sliding sideways down features, hitting rollers. The hybrid profile gives it more edge hold than its playful character might suggest, making it a capable board on groomers too. Rome's construction is reliable and the board holds up well through heavy park use.
why_recommended: Your intermediate ability and preference for a playful, fun ride are well matched to the Artifact. It's versatile enough to ride anywhere while rewarding the kind of freestyle mindset that your terrain preferences suggest.

description: A versatile park-oriented twin with medium flex and full camber for consistent pop. Well-suited to riders who want jib performance without sacrificing edge hold.
full_description: The Salomon Huck Knife bridges the gap between pure park boards and all-mountain freestyle boards exceptionally well. Full camber gives it consistent, reliable pop that soft rocker park boards simply can't match. Medium flex makes it playful enough for rails and jibs while being stiff enough to handle some speed. The true twin shape means every trick and every landing feels the same regardless of direction. Salomon's construction quality is excellent and the base is fast and durable. It's the park board for riders who want to progress beyond beginner features into more serious terrain.
why_recommended: Your park focus combined with intermediate-advanced ability and a balanced/playful feel preference makes the Huck Knife a strong match. The camber profile gives you the pop to progress into bigger features, while the medium flex keeps things accessible.

description: A well-rounded all-mountain board with a hybrid camber-rocker profile. Confident on hardpack and forgiving in variable snow — a true all-rounder.
full_description: The Salomon Assassin is the definition of a well-rounded all-mountain board. The hybrid Quadratic Camber profile — camber between the bindings, rocker in the nose and tail — delivers versatility across snow conditions that neither pure camber nor pure rocker can achieve alone. Medium-stiff flex means it handles steep, demanding terrain while remaining accessible for riders who aren't at the absolute top of their game. Strong edge hold keeps it planted on hardpack. The directional twin shape offers a hint of setback for variable snow while maintaining enough symmetry for switch riding.
why_recommended: The Assassin's all-mountain versatility and balanced character directly match your riding style and preferred feel. The medium-stiff flex and strong edge hold address multiple terrain needs simultaneously, making it an efficient single-board choice for your ability and preferences.

description: YES's entry-level all-mountain freestyle board. Soft and forgiving with a hybrid profile — perfect for progressing riders who want versatility without intimidation.
full_description: The YES. Basic is exactly what beginning-intermediate riders need: a board that helps you progress without fighting you. The hybrid profile combines just enough rocker to be forgiving and fun with enough camber to give you grip and pop as your skills develop. Soft-to-medium flex means mistakes don't get punished harshly and learning to link turns feels natural and encouraging. The true twin shape works going both directions, useful for riders starting to explore switch. YES. boards have a reputation for quality construction above their price point, and the Basic lives up to that.
why_recommended: For beginner-intermediate riders wanting a versatile, forgiving all-mountain experience, the YES. Basic ticks every box. Its soft hybrid flex and playful character match your current ability and preferred feel, and it'll remain relevant as you progress.

description: A highly accessible beginner-intermediate board. Super soft flex and full rocker make learning to turn and absorbing terrain mistakes easy and forgiving.
full_description: The K2 Raygun is one of the most approachable beginner-intermediate boards on the market. The super soft flex means the board bends easily in response to your movements, making turns feel accessible rather than intimidating. Full rocker keeps the edges lifted and prevents catching — beginners' biggest source of crashes. The true twin shape means it doesn't have a wrong direction. K2's manufacturing quality is excellent at this price point. The Raygun is a board that makes learning enjoyable rather than frustrating, which is the most important thing a beginner board can do.
why_recommended: Your beginner-intermediate ability and desire for a playful, forgiving ride are exactly what the Raygun is designed for. The super soft flex and full rocker will help you develop your technique without the board fighting you at every turn.

description: A stiff directional freeride board built for charging hard on and off piste. Full camber and strong edge hold make it a confident performer in technical terrain.
full_description: The K2 Manifest is a directional freeride board built for riders who want to charge hard and go fast. Full camber and stiff flex create a board that demands commitment. The 12mm setback keeps weight naturally over the tail, helping it track straight at high speed and providing float in variable snow. Strong edge hold and a fast sintered base make it confident on steep groomed terrain and off-piste in firmer conditions. K2 has refined the construction over multiple seasons resulting in a board that feels precise and connected. Intermediate-advanced riders with freeride ambition will find it very rewarding.
why_recommended: The Manifest's freeride character, stiff flex, and aggressive edge hold match your riding style, ability, and preferred feel precisely. The directional shape and setback stance are optimised for the demanding terrain your inputs indicate.

description: Arbor's dedicated beginner board. Ultra-soft flex and full rocker make it the most forgiving board on the mountain — built to help new riders find their feet with confidence.
full_description: The Arbor Foundation is a dedicated beginner board designed to make learning as comfortable and confidence-inspiring as possible. The ultra-soft flex bends forgivingly in response to even small movements, making steering and controlling a snowboard feel intuitive rather than confrontational. Full continuous rocker keeps the edges away from the snow, virtually eliminating edge catches — the leading cause of beginner falls. Arbor's commitment to sustainability means the board uses Forest Stewardship Council certified wood. The Foundation is not the most exciting board on the mountain — it's supposed to get you riding with confidence, and it does that better than almost anything else at its price point.
why_recommended: As a beginner, the Foundation gives you exactly what you need: maximum forgiveness, minimum interference, and a confidence-building experience. The ultra-soft flex and full rocker are tailored specifically to your ability level, and you won't outgrow it until you're ready for something more demanding.
